channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Middle East tensions push oil prices up and global markets down
item.description: Global financial markets are facing turbulence as the conflict in the Middle East escalates. Oil prices surged dramatically, with Brent crude reaching $114.36 per barrel and US crude hitting $115.11, ...

item.title: Trump endorsement likely to influence Texas GOP Senate race, betting markets indicate
item.description: In the recent Texas Republican primary, incumbent Senator John Cornyn came first against Attorney General Ken Paxton, though neither candidate achieved a majority, triggering a potential runoff over t...

item.title: Gradual Recovery Observed at South Africa’s Major Ports
item.description: South African ports are showing signs of recovery after a sharp decline in ship arrivals between 2019 and 2021. This drop, from 9,882 vessels in 2019 to 8,453 in 2021, was largely due to the Covid-19 ...

item.title: EAM S Jaishankar to Update Parliament on West Asia Situation and Indian Citizens’ Safety
item.description: External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar is scheduled to brief both Houses of Parliament on Monday regarding the ongoing situation in West Asia, focusing on tensions arising from the US-Iran conflict. H...

item.title: Mojtaba Khamenei Appointed Iran’s Supreme Leader Amid US Caution and Israeli Threats
item.description: Tehran has officially confirmed Mojtaba Khamenei as the new Supreme Leader of Iran, following the legacy of his late father, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. His appointment is seen as a continuation of Iran’s...

item.title: Philippines Seeks Non-Permanent Seat in UN Security Council Amid Global Challenges
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. visited New York from March 9 to 10, 2026, for a major push to secure the Philippines a non-permanent seat in the UN Security Council for the 2027–2028 term. This visit ...

item.title: Governors of Abia and Cross River Honour Women on International Women’s Day
item.description: For International Women’s Day wey dey celebrated every 8th of March, Abia State Governor, Alex Otti, and Cross River State Governor, Bassey Otu, don take time dey appreciate di women dem for dia respe...

item.title: Redmi Note 15 Pro+ 5G Offers Strong Features for Content Creators Without Breaking the Bank
item.description: Xiaomi’s latest midrange smartphone, the Redmi Note 15 Pro+ 5G, has been reviewed as an excellent option for content creators who want solid performance without spending too much. The phone offers 5G ...

item.title: US economic instability and rising oil prices under Trump may affect Australian superannuation
item.description: The US economy is showing clear signs of stagflation, a combination of rising unemployment and inflation reminiscent of the 1970s. This situation has been exacerbated by geopolitical tensions followin...

item.title: Zimbabwe lithium export ban disrupts Chinese battery supply chain
item.description: On 25 February 2026, Zimbabwe suddenly announce say e go suspend all lithium concentrate exports immediately, even the ones wey already dey in transit. This kain move don shake Chinese industrial circ...

item.title: Stakeholders urge government to promote Gwadar Port for transhipment amid disruptions from Iran conflict
item.description: Amid the ongoing Iran war that has disrupted regional shipping routes and caused higher insurance costs, shipping stakeholders and government officials met in Karachi on March 6, 2026, to discuss ways...

item.title: Key ISIS-linked Leader Killed in Military Operation in North Cotabato
item.description: Authorities reported that Emarudin Kulaw, also known as Alpha King, a high-ranking leader of the Dawlah Islamiyah (DI) group linked to ISIS, was killed during a military operation in Barangay Tinimbac...

item.title: FAAN MD Kuku says Nigeria must fix business environment and infrastructure to become aviation hub
item.description: The Managing Director of the Federal Airports Authority of Nigeria (FAAN), Olubunmi Kuku, don talk say Nigeria no go fit reach true airport hub status if dem no fix better business environment and boo...

item.title: RJ Barrett scores 31 as Raptors defeat Mavericks 122-92
item.description: TORONTO – RJ Barrett, hailing from Mississauga, Ont., delivered a season-high 31 points to guide the Toronto Raptors to a decisive 122-92 victory over the Dallas Mavericks on Sunday. Barrett also cont...

item.title: Satellite Radar Monitoring Could Reduce High-Risk Bridge Classifications by Detecting Early Deformations Globally
item.description: Researchers from the University of Houston and international collaborators have demonstrated that satellite-based radar technology, specifically Multi-Temporal Interferometric Synthetic Aperture Radar...

item.title: SA Government Keeps Details of Delegation to UN Women’s Summit Confidential
item.description: As the 70th session of the Commission on the Status of Women (CSW70) kicks off in New York, the South African government has not disclosed the full size or cost of its delegation. Requests for details...

item.title: SAFA NEC meeting disrupted as tensions escalate between officials
item.description: During a heated SAFA NEC meeting at Safa House on Saturday, tensions escalated to near-physical confrontations, requiring security intervention. The dispute arose over the order of agenda items, inclu...

item.title: Call for urgent probe into irregular issuance of Kenyan passports and illegal recruitment
item.description: The Kenyan government is facing serious allegations over the illegal sale of passports to foreigners and the secret recruitment of jobless youths to fight in the Russia-Ukraine conflict. Opposition le...

item.title: Kasasa Island residents concerned over Chinese land purchases amid Japan-China geopolitical strains
item.description: Kasasa Island, a small, largely abandoned island in Japan's Seto Inland Sea with only seven registered residents, has become a focal point for growing tensions between Japan and China. The island, kno...

item.title: Lessons on Time and Discipline from National Artist Rio Alma
item.description: National Artist for Literature Virgilio S. Almario, also known as Rio Alma, exemplifies the value of time both in personal discipline and cultural wisdom. Known for always arriving early to classes, m...
